mood.label: RISK-OFF
mood.summary: A global tech and semiconductor sell-off is driving a risk-off sentiment, with US futures pointing to a sharply lower open. Heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are adding to market jitters.

indicesNarrative: US index futures are sharply lower, led by a significant drop in the Nasdaq, as a global sell-off in semiconductor and AI-related stocks continues. The VIX has risen to a nine-day high, indicating increasing investor anxiety. Crude oil is trading higher due to escalating tensions in the Middle East.
sectorsNarrative: The technology sector, particularly semiconductors, is experiencing a significant downturn, dragging the broader market lower. This follows a period of strong performance, suggesting profit-taking and concerns about the sustainability of high valuations in the AI space.
industryNarrative: Semiconductor and AI-related industries are leading the market decline. Storage chip and optical communications sectors are also seeing significant pre-market losses.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: The current market is not conducive for buying. It's a time to protect capital and wait for high-quality setups to emerge in a confirmed uptrend.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: There is a clear rotation out of technology and semiconductor stocks. It's important to see where institutional money is flowing next before making any aggressive moves.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: The market context is weak, and this is not the time to be aggressive. Focus on A-grade setups and wait for the market to show signs of strength before deploying capital.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Avoid chasing breakdowns. Wait for prices to stabilize and form clean pivots before considering any entries. Volume confirmation will be key.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: Stick to your trading plan and risk management rules. Honor your stops and avoid emotional decision-making in this volatile environment.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: The momentum has shifted to the downside. This is not the environment for chasing breakouts. Be patient and wait for the market to bottom and new leaders to emerge.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: The market is in a risk-off mode, driven by a global tech sell-off. The best course of action is to wait on the sidelines, observe the price action, and preserve capital. Do not force trades in this environment.

alert: Global tech and semiconductor sell-off is accelerating, with Nasdaq futures pointing to a sharply lower open. Exercise extreme caution.
